statement: Observatory demonstrates whether procedural memory derived from a verified failure changes a warm rerun for the right, attributable reason.
The flagship slice is WorkItem to cold failure to GPT-5.6 memory proposal to deterministic admission and GraphReFly application to warm rerun to replay UI and evidence bundle.
GPT-5.6 performs semantic interpretation and structured proposal generation; an independent deterministic verifier owns pass or fail.
Cold and warm executions use the same canonical WorkItem input; relevant admitted memory makes the warm run avoid the prior failure route and pass verification.
Proposal-only, admission-rejected, irrelevant-applied, and wrong-scope-applied controls cannot manufacture success.
Every applied or rejected memory record has exact trace attribution and a reason code.
A fixture-backed replay works without an API key and a live GPT-5.6 path demonstrates actual model use.
A first-time judge can understand the problem, changed decision, verifier result, and proof within the under-three-minute demo.
GraphReFly Stack is a semantic stacked-diff layer above Git that tells developers whether an agent-generated change is still valid under the architecture it was planned against.

The flagship slice runs task to GPT-5.6 ChangePlan to real stacked commits and commit-indexed GraphBlueprint snapshots to a textually clean but semantically invalidating architecture change to selective replan, deterministic gate recovery, and high-level review.
GPT-5.6 proposes semantic plans, claims, implementation, explanations, and replans; deterministic code owns snapshot derivation, scope validation, freshness, impact, checks, and GateResult.

The flagship proof keeps an unaffected upstream work unit valid, invalidates only stale downstream work despite a clean Git rebase, and admits the affected work only after a fresh selective replan passes deterministic checks.
Every material validity, invalidation, and recovery claim resolves to exact witnesses, reason codes, and reproducible check outputs rather than model confidence or commit prose.
A reviewer can understand intent, architectural delta, affected capabilities, stack dependencies, unresolved risk, and merge readiness before choosing whether to inspect the raw code diff.

The Build Week MVP implements only the GraphReFly BlueprintProvider, the canonical CLI, a local review UI, one flagship scenario, and manual semantic approval; other graph providers, MCP, hosted CI, and automatic merge remain deferred.
Authentication, teams, and collaboration are outside the MVP.
Replacing Git, GitHub, Graphite, or other stacked-diff transport and review mechanics is outside the MVP.
Nx, Turborepo, TypeScript project-reference, and other non-GraphReFly BlueprintProvider implementations are outside the MVP.
An MCP server, GitHub App, and hosted required-check integration are outside the MVP.
Automatic semantic approval, automatic merge, and unattended production mutation are outside the MVP.
Reimplementing or vendoring GraphReFly is prohibited.
Multiple unrelated demo scenarios and a production database are outside the MVP.
